description: '''Ikorodo music''' {{Audio|LL-Q33578 (ibo)-Vivian Amalachukwu-Ikorodo music.wav|Listen|help=no}} is a [[Igbo Music|traditional Igbo folk music]]. The genre is originated from culture of the Nsukka people in [[Enugu State]], Nigeria. The traditional folk genre includes many arrays of performance practices as a result isn't solely aural as it intertwines with visual expressions. Th genre features a set of [[Horn (instrument)|horns]] known as ''opi'', revered for its melodic resonance and plays pivotal role in orchestrating complex rhythmic patterns through the application of hocket technique.
